title.rendered: How To Handle A Leaky Roof In Winter

content.rendered:
When you have issues with your roof, you simply deal with it by getting the help of a professional. But during the winter, your hands may be tied. You have to deal with snow, strong winds, slippery conditions, and other issues that make repairs difficult. Here are some things you can do to deal with a leaky roof during this challenging period.

1. Identify the Cause of the Leak

The first step is to deal with what’s causing the leak. Do you have shingles that have become loose or damaged? Are you having issues in your attic due to condensation building up and the moisture damaging the roof from the inside-out?

Find a professional contractor that focuses on roofing in Calgary. You should get a clear diagnosis so that you can make the necessary adjustments and understand what options are available to you.

2. Create an Environment that Reduces moisture

If you do find that the leak is caused by excessive humidity in your attic, there are several things that you can do to reduce it. Start by lowering your humidistat if you have one installed. Then open your vents to better distribute the humid air throughout the home. Finally, run fans in the attic, bathroom, and kitchen to help dry out humid air.

3. Wait for the Right Conditions for Repairs

During the winter, you need to get repairs in when the conditions are right. Keep track of weather forecasts. You want to time your repairs when there’s no rain, no strong winds, and no snowfall. If repairs are absolutely necessary, you have to work with your contractor to get everything fixed under good weather conditions.

4. Find Companies that Specialize in Winter Repairs

Some companies specialize in winter repairs for roofing in Calgary. They will use various tools and methods to repair your home during the winter. For example, they may use tarps and other protective tools to protect your roof from moisture while they’re working on it. They will also have experience completing the job very quickly so that repairs are done before lousy weather makes things difficult.

These are just some things that you can do to deal with a leaky roof during the winter season. While repairs are ideal in warmer and dry seasons, they may be required because the leak can lead to extensive damage.

At City Roofing and Exteriors, we take pride in our work and by serving Calgarians. We work hard to assist our customers in providing proper roofing systems to meet your budget and needs. We guarantee that you will be satisfied with our work, give us a call at (403) 608-9933 or email us at info@calgarycityroofing.com.
